title: rubus × hollandrei
intro: Rubus × hollandrei, also known as the hybrid black raspberry, is a cross between the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the purple raspberry (Rubus idaeus), resulting in a unique and hardy plant with a mix of characteristics from its parents. The leaves and stems of Rubus × hollandrei have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them useful for soothing sore throats, reducing fever, and treating digestive issues. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and providing anti-cancer properties due to its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid. Rubus × hollandrei can be prepared into herbal teas, jams, and extracts, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits and medicinal properties.
